questions.question: Which website allows builders to access daily reward credits for building, according to Jesse Pollak?
questions.explanation: Jesse Pollak's post on 2026-02-28 mentions daily reward credits to build on neynar.com/studio.

questions.question: On February 24, 2026, Jesse Pollak expected instability at 12:00 PM UTC due to what event?
options: Shard 0 cutting over to an updated validator set
A DDoS attack on the Farcaster network
questions.explanation: Jesse Pollak specifically noted shard 0 cutting over to the updated validator set as the cause for expected instability.

questions.question: What games did Jesse Pollak compare the 'effort reward cycle' of coding agents to?
questions.explanation: Jesse mentions pulling all-nighters playing DoTA or LoL at USC's GamePipe Lab and compared the rewarding feeling of coding agents to these games.

questions.question: What is the name of the 'valley' farms that Jesse Pollak's kids are now asking for?
questions.explanation: Jesse's post on 2026-02-15 says his kids are asking for their own /fruitling-valley farms.

questions.question: Jesse Pollak mentioned significant changes at the Farcaster database layer on February 5, 2026. What was the expected impact window?
questions.explanation: The post specified database layer changes between noon - 1PM PST on that day.

questions.question: Jesse Pollak referenced a humanoid robot when discussing the OpenClaw agent. Which model was it?
questions.explanation: Jesse asked if anyone had given an OpenClaw agent access to a humanoid like the G1.

questions.question: What fictional item did Jesse Pollak use to describe the potential of LLMs, agents, and folding displays?
questions.explanation: Jesse stated that Tom Riddle’s diary will no longer be fiction due to LLMs and folding displays.

questions.question: How much did global p95 latency decrease for @mcilroyc over a 24-hour period according to Jesse Pollak?
questions.explanation: Jesse shared that @mcilroyc achieved 50% less global p95 latency.

questions.question: What new feature was added to neynar.com/studio in January 2026 to help with real estate?
questions.explanation: Jesse announced 'We put Studio on web. for some more real estate' on 2026-01-23.

questions.question: Jesse Pollak gave 'massive props' to which team for making Farcaster what it is today?
questions.explanation: Jesse specifically thanked @dwr, @v, and the 'amazing team at Merkle'.

questions.question: Jesse Pollak expressed surprise that many people were discovering which AI product late in the adoption timeline?
questions.explanation: Jesse was surprised how many people were discovering Claude and other Anthropic products late.

questions.question: In March 2024, the official Base account reported that revenue had doubled. What was the reason given?
questions.explanation: The Base official account stated: 'last week we doubled our revenue and I have no idea why'.

questions.question: A 2022 post from the Base official account compared early internet journalism to what?
questions.explanation: The post linked to an article comparing early internet journalism to crypto journalism.

questions.question: What is Dan Romero's current policy regarding 15-minute call requests?
questions.explanation: Dan Romero stated: 'I'm pretty busy with the new gig, so I'm auto-decline.'

questions.question: What 48oz beverage bucket does Dan Romero want to 'drinkmog' matchas with?
questions.explanation: Dan Romero mentioned drinking matchas with 'one of these 48oz beverage buckets from dunkin'.

questions.question: According to Dan Romero, what is the best website to order new clothing items based on saved sizes?
questions.explanation: Dan Romero mentioned he is a big fan of Proper Cloth and it is the best website to order based on saved sizes.

questions.question: Whose 'Fast' list did Dan Romero suggest adding a new entry to in February 2026?
questions.explanation: Dan Romero linked to patrickcollison.com/fast and said 'time to add one to the list'.

questions.question: In his Farcaster post-mortem, what retention metric did Dan Romero say would have made them 'thrilled' to spend money on acquisition?
options: 1 out of 10 users after a year
8 out of 10 users after a week
questions.explanation: Dan wrote: 'If 3 out of every 10 users that signed up continued to use Farcaster after 30 days, we would have been thrilled to spend the money'.

questions.question: What analogy did Dan Romero use to describe the philosophy of raising money when timing is good?
options: When they are passing hors d'oeuvres, take two
Make hay while the sun shines
Strike while the iron is hot
A rising tide lifts all boats
questions.explanation: Dan shared a quip from an operator: 'When they are passing hors d'oeuvres, take two.'

questions.question: Which two technical achievements did Dan Romero highlight as 'impressive' despite Farcaster's failure to find PMF?
questions.explanation: Dan stated: 'Hubs and Snapchain were / are an impressive technical achievement'.

questions.question: Brian Armstrong recently expressed joy about switching back to which terminal emulator?

questions.question: Brian Armstrong mentioned using which database in his next side project?
questions.explanation: Brian Armstrong posted: 'lmao spacetimedb is nuts gonna try using it in my next side project'.

questions.question: What is the primary method Brian Armstrong suggests for users to reach him lately?
questions.explanation: Brian Armstrong repeatedly uses the phrase 'want to reach me? beep me' and provides beep.works links.

questions.question: What animal sound/name did Brian Armstrong use to describe his 'HOWLER' on Feb 14?
questions.explanation: Brian Armstrong posted 'awooooooooooooo 🐺 rate my HOWLER'.

questions.question: Brian Armstrong warned about 'homograph attacks' using what type of characters?
questions.explanation: Brian warned about using identical-looking Cyrillic characters in curl commands.

questions.question: On what date did Jesse Pollak tweet 'skill issue has a whole new meaning now'?
questions.explanation: The post date for the 'skill issue' tweet is 2026-02-27.

questions.question: According to Jesse Pollak, what is 'the Neynar way' for handling bugs?
options: Detailed linear ticket, magic, test + review, ship
Fix it on Friday, ship on Monday
Ignore it until it becomes a feature
questions.explanation: Jesse described 'The Neynar way' as: 1. Detailed linear ticket 2. magic 3. test + review 4. ship 5. reply back.

questions.explanation: Jesse planned upgrades to authentication and user management between 8-8:30am PST.

questions.explanation: The post simply says: 'from 1 to N - build more!'.

How to find a gym buddy
